assertions.reason: The hosted eyawa.me frontend loaded successfully as DualVM Lending and showed no critical console errors after a clean reload.

steps.action: Open the hosted URL in the dedicated browser session
steps.observed: Page title was 'DualVM Lending' and the hosted page rendered successfully
No console/error output was emitted after clearing diagnostics and reloading

steps.action: Check the hosted URL over HTTP

evidence.consoleErrors: none after clear+reload on the hosted page
network: browser network log observed GET http://eyawa.me/dualvm-lending/ (document) and asset fetches

assertions.reason: The local current-code frontend displayed a visible RainbowKit-style 'Connect Wallet' button on initial load. The hosted build is older and does not expose this control.

steps.observed: DualVM Lending loaded from the local dev server

steps.expected: A wallet connect button is visible on page load
steps.observed: A visible 'Connect Wallet' button was present at the top of the page

evidence.consoleErrors: non-critical local dev diagnostics only: Vite connect/debug logs, Lit dev-mode warning, and Reown/WalletConnect remote-config 403/400 warnings; the page still rendered and the button was visible
network: browser network log observed GET http://localhost:5173/ (document) and Vite-served JS/CSS assets

assertions.reason: The local current-code frontend populated the market snapshot/read layer with pool total assets, utilization, oracle price, oracle freshness, and version metadata.

steps.action: Load the local frontend read layer
steps.observed: The page showed Pool total assets 23,900 USDC-test, Utilization 17.60%, Oracle price 100 USDC-test / WPAS, and Oracle freshness fresh

steps.action: Inspect version metadata shown with the read layer
steps.expected: Active/latest market version is visible if present
steps.observed: The page showed Active market version v1 and Latest market version v3

evidence.consoleErrors: same non-critical local dev diagnostics noted for VAL-UI-001; no app-crashing error prevented the read layer from rendering
network: browser network log showed repeated POST https://eth-rpc-testnet.polkadot.io/ reads while the snapshot populated

assertions.reason: Observer mode accepted a borrower address and displayed the borrower readout, including available-to-borrow and health factor.

steps.action: Enter borrower address 0x31FA19B35fdBD96f381A0be838799ca40978D080 into the observer input
steps.observed: The input accepted the address and Track address completed without error

steps.expected: Health factor and available-to-borrow are shown
steps.observed: The page showed Tracked address 0x31FA…D080, Available to borrow 0 USDC-test, and Health factor ∞

evidence.consoleErrors: same non-critical local dev diagnostics noted for VAL-UI-001; observer mode still returned values
network: browser network log showed public-RPC POST reads during observer refresh

assertions.reason: Using frontend-only evidence, the current local frontend reflects governed market version information by displaying both the active and latest market version values.

steps.action: Inspect the local current-code frontend for governance/version readouts
steps.expected: Frontend reflects governed market version information without performing governance writes
steps.observed: The page displayed Active market version v1 and Latest market version v3

evidence.consoleErrors: same non-critical local dev diagnostics noted for VAL-UI-001
network: browser network log showed live public-RPC reads backing the rendered version/read-layer data

frictions.description: The hosted eyawa.me build loaded successfully but is an older observer-only deployment. It does not expose the visible Connect Wallet button or active/latest market version fields that appear in the local current-code build.
frictions.resolution: Used the hosted URL only for VAL-UI-010 as requested, then switched the same browser session to http://localhost:5173/ to confirm the latest UI shape for VAL-UI-001, VAL-UI-003, VAL-UI-004, and VAL-CROSS-002.

frictions.description: The local dev build emits non-blocking WalletConnect/Reown remote-config 403/400 console diagnostics plus dev-mode warnings.
frictions.resolution: Recorded them as non-critical because the page rendered, the connect button was visible, the read layer populated, and observer mode returned values.

summary: Hosted frontend accessibility passed on eyawa.me. The current local frontend confirmed the connect button, populated market snapshot, observer readout, and governed market version display using one browser session and no on-chain writes.
